rules.title: AWS CDK — 2025 Elevated Standards
rules.description: Enforces secure, testable, multi-account IaC with CI gates for AWS CDK deployments.
rules.justification: Modern CDK practices ensure security, maintainability, and scalability across environments.

items: **TypeScript strict** only; disallow implicit any; emit declarations for constructs. Enforce via `tsconfig` and lint rules.
Node engines pinned (>=20.x) across apps; keep uniform with root engines.
Keep **app entry** in `bin/` and constructs in `lib/`. Provide **scripts** for `synth`, `diff`, `deploy`, `destroy`.
Turn on **feature flags/context** in `cdk.json` to get modern behavior and validations. (You already carry many safety flags.)
Separate **accounts**: `dev`, `stage`, `prod`. Bootstrap each with a unique **qualifier** and permission boundaries.
Name stacks and assets with `{app}-{env}`; never share stateful resources across envs.

frontmatter.description: Elevated 2025 CDK standards (any repo). Enforces secure, testable, multi-account IaC with CI gates.

rules.title: Freshworks API Request Validation
rules.description: All API requests to Freshworks must include proper authentication headers and validate responses before processing.
rules.justification: Ensures secure and reliable communication with Freshworks CRM platform, preventing data corruption and authentication failures.

content.text: All Freshworks API calls must include proper authentication and error handling.

content.caption: Proper API Request with Authentication

frontmatter.description: Freshworks API request validation and authentication

rules.title: TypeScript Strict Mode Compliance
rules.description: All TypeScript files must use strict mode settings to ensure type safety and catch potential runtime errors at compile time.
rules.justification: TypeScript strict mode prevents common JavaScript errors and ensures type safety throughout the application.

examples.title: Strict TypeScript Configuration
Proper Type Annotations
examples.description: Shows proper type annotations and null checking

rules.title: React Error Boundary Implementation
rules.description: All React components that render user data must be wrapped in error boundaries to prevent application crashes and provide graceful error handling.
rules.justification: Error boundaries prevent cascading failures and provide better user experience when components fail.
